title: olea europaea subsp. oleaster
intro: Olea europaea subsp. oleaster, also known as the wild olive or oleaster, is a subspecies of the olive tree native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of olea europaea subsp. oleaster include improving cardiovascular health, reducing the risk of certain cancers, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
